original_abstract: This research evaluates the software quality of the e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia (e-learning.unmaha.ac.id) using the McCall Method. To provide a holistic view of the platform’s performance, the study assesses several key metrics: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability. The evaluation yielded an overall quality score of 79.6%, placing the system in the "Good" category. While the results demonstrate high performance in terms of system integrity and reliability, the study identifies specific weaknesses in information correctness and ease of maintenance. Based on these findings, we offer targeted recommendations to improve content accuracy, refine the user interface for a more intuitive experience, and streamline backend maintainability to ensure long-term sustainability. These insights serve as a strategic foundation for developing a more responsive and effective e-learning environment, further strengthening Universitas Mahakarya Asia’s commitment to high-quality digital education.
humanized_abstract: The Universitas Mahakarya Asia e-learning system is equipped with a host of applications designed to facilitate online learning for students, teachers and lecturers. This study uses the McCall Method to analyse the quality of the software which forms the basis of the e-learning system. Many researchers have shown the importance of analysing the e-learning system quality to determine the weaknesses and strengths, so that appropriate measures can be taken to improve its performance. In this study, several aspects of e-learning system performance have been examined, namely: accuracy, reliability, speed, consistency, usability and maintainability. From the analysis, the overall quality value of the e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia (e-learning.unmaha.ac.id) obtained is 79.6% and it falls into the "Good" category. From the results, it can be seen that the system consist of high consistence, reliability, but has shortcomings in terms of information accuracy and maintenance ease. Recommendations are given in an attempt to improve the quality of the system. It is hoped that these recommendations would help to enhance information accuracy, user interface and backend maintenance in order to improve the performance of the online learning system and would be a strategic step for improving the quality of e-learning system of Universitas Mahakarya Asia so as to support the University’s efforts to develop quality online education.
